steps.title: Match the coefficient condition
steps.content: A linear equation in one variable has no solution when the $x$-coefficients match but the constants are different. Match the $x$-coefficients first.

steps.title: Confirm by expanding
steps.content: Expand the left side: $$4(kx+11)=4kx+44.$$ The equation is $$4kx+44=\frac{60}{19}x+40.$$ For no solution, match the $x$-coefficients: $$4k=\frac{60}{19}$$ $$k=\frac{60}{76}=\frac{15}{19}\approx0.7895.$$

steps.title: Check the contradiction
steps.content: With $k=\frac{15}{19}$, the $x$-terms match, but the constants are $44$ and $40$. After subtracting the matching $x$-terms, the equation becomes $44=40$, which is impossible. Therefore the equation has no solution, and the answer is 15/19.
